Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-18 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
# Market Analysis: momentum-ranker Today's data reveals two exceptionally strong momentum performers dominating the rankings. TYRA leads with a composite score of 97.7 (rank #1), driven by extraordinary consistency: it's up 254% over one year while maintaining all four SMA gates (above 30/50/100/200 day averages) and posting perfect ratio passes across timeframes. TNGX follows as a close second with an extreme 858% one-year return, though it shows one missing ratio data point and a slightly lower bad-SPY resilience score (1.15 vs TYRA's 1.45), suggesting marginally less stability during market downturns. Both stocks demonstrate rare alignment across multiple timeframes—they beat SPY by 238% and 841% respectively over one year—but TNGX's explosive gains come with higher concentration risk, evidenced by its weaker correlation diversity and no identifiable top correlated asset in the data provided. The divergence in bad-SPY scores (which measure resilience when the broad market declines) indicates TYRA offers more consistent momentum even under stress conditions, making it the more resilient choice despite TNGX's larger absolute returns.
